div.h3: Implementation Notes for File System Engineers:

ol.li: Lock Granularity: This diagram assumes a VFS-level global lock. For production, use per-directory inode locks or Range Locks.
Atomic Bitops: The bitmap modification bit 10=1 should use set_bit() atomic instructions if the lock is not held.
FUSE/Kernel Integration: Ensure mkdir call returns EEXIST if dir_lookup finds the entry after acquiring the lock.
Journaling: All metadata writes shown must be wrapped in a jbd2 transaction handle to ensure consistency after power-loss.

g.text: |'md **Red**: Concurrent write conflict / Data corruption'|
|'md **INVARIANT:** No two directory entries may map to the same Inode index.'|
|'md **RACE POINT:** Both threads read the same state. Inode 10 is seen as free (0).'|
|'md **CORRUPTION:** Inode 10 metadata overwritten. /dir1 is now orphaned.'|
|'md **BLOCKING:** Thread B suspended in futex_wait queue.'|
